title: lithocarpus pseudovestitus
intro: Lithocarpus pseudovestitus is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, commonly known as a type of oak tree, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The bark and leaves of the Lithocarpus pseudovestitus plant have been traditionally used in medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lithocarpus pseudovestitus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Lithocarpus pseudovestitus is typically prepared as a decoction by boiling the bark and leaves in water, or as a tincture by soaking the plant material in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
